subject: [PATCH v2 1/1] mm: reinstate unconditional writeback start in balance_dirty_pages()

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that the patch has been queued in mm.git's mm-hotfixes-unstable branch for testing and review, indicating a positive reception of the patch's urgency due to the severe performance regression it addresses.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks, I queued this in mm.git's mm-hotfixes-unstable branch for test
and review.  This starts it on the track to mainline before the 7.0
release.

I assume we want to be prompt with this because the fuse regression
sounds pretty horrid.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch looks good and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag from himself.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the unconditional writeback start was reinstated due to a combination of the original comment and his own initial misreading, and he found the patch good after reevaluation.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for the fix. This was a combination of the old comment and my
overly hasty read of the existing dirty > bg_thresh &&
!writeback_in_progress() block. The patch looks good to me.

Acked-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>

2026-03-26.patch_summary: This patch reinstates unconditional writeback start in balance_dirty_pages() to prevent performance regressions for strictlimited BDIs and memcg setups. The removal of this logic caused stalls when IO was throttled while waiting for dirty pages to be written back, but no writeback was running. This is particularly problematic for fuse buffered writes, which saw a drop from 1400 MiB/s to 2000 KiB/s performance. The patch adds the unconditional writeback start back in to ensure that writeback is always running when IO needs to be throttled.
